author: Christian Reynoso
title: Curried Cabbage Stew with Turmeric-Chile Butter Toasts
ingredients: 2 cups diced sweet potato (from about 1 medium sweet potato)
1 small head (about 10 ounces) of Savoy or green cabbage, cored and roughly chopped
1 (14-ounce) can white beans, drained and rinsed
¼ teaspoon crushed red chile flakes
1 baguette, split in half lengthwise and toasted with olive oil
2 cups diced sweet potato (from about 1 medium sweet potato)
1 small head (about 10 ounces) of Savoy or green cabbage, cored and roughly chopped
1 (14-ounce) can white beans, drained and rinsed
¼ teaspoon crushed red chile flakes
1 baguette, split in half lengthwise and toasted with olive oil

instructions:
Heat the olive oil in a large size pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Once the oil is warm, add the sweet potato, onion, garlic and ginger. Season with salt and pepper and stir. Cover with a lid and cook, lifting the lid to stir occasionally until the potato is fork tender and the onion is beginning to caramelize on the edges, about 8 to 12 minutes. Stir the curry powder into the vegetables and follow with the cabbage. Season the cabbage with salt, then stir and cook it until it wilts to half its volume, about 3 minutes. Pour in the stock and bring to a simmer. Simmer until the cabbage has completely wilted and some white parts turn a bit yellow, about 7 minutes. Add the white beans and simmer for 1 more minute. Turn the heat off and season with salt to taste.
Meanwhile, add the butter, turmeric and chile to a small bowl and mix together with a fork. Finely grate in about half the lemon zest and season with salt.
To serve, ladle the stew into bowls. Break the baguette into smaller pieces — I prefer large pieces, about ¼ of the total baguette — and toast. Spread the butter on the toast and place in the bowl with the stew.

description: This gently spiced cabbage, sweet potato and white bean stew hits the sweet spot between nourishing and indulgent, especially with turmeric-chile butter toast o
